insight: ChatControl's Discord integration requires the DiscordSRV plugin. Users must disable DiscordSRV's built-in chat relay by setting `DiscordChatChannelMinecraftToDiscord: false` in DiscordSRV's `config.yml`, otherwise messages get duplicated. ChatControl checks this on startup and logs a warning if it's still enabled (see ChatControl.java line ~247).

Users commonly place `/chc a image <file> <height> <message>` inside a format part's `Message:` key expecting it to render an image. This prints the command text literally because `Message:` only displays text — it never executes commands. The correct approach is to use `Image_File`, `Image_Head`, or `Image_Url` keys on the format part. `/chc a image` is exclusively for one-time broadcast announcements via console or in-game command.

Auto_Mode on Folia does NOT require PacketEvents. Auto_Mode uses dispatchCommand('/tell ...') which flows through Foundation's BukkitPlayer.performPlayerCommand0(), which on Folia uses player.getScheduler().run() for correct entity-thread scheduling. The Folia disable check in Settings.java was outdated and can be removed entirely. The PacketEvents-based conversation fix in BukkitPacketListener is for a completely different feature (Bukkit's Conversation API with player.isConversing()/acceptConversationInput()), NOT for Auto_Mode.

When multiple message groups use 'require sender perm' for rank-based messages (e.g. premium, vip, default) and a player has inherited permissions matching all groups, Stop_On_First_Match correctly prevents duplicates in the main player loop. However, the sender-fallback path in PlayerMessage.filter() (line ~849: 'if !pickedMessage && wrappedSender != null') fires per group and did NOT check messageReceivers, causing the sender to receive duplicate messages from each matching group. PR #3513 fixes this by adding a messageReceivers + Stop_On_First_Match check to the fallback condition.

InteractiveChat compatibility (#3682): default `Chat_Listener_Priority: HIGH-MODERN` runs ChatControl on Paper's AsyncChatEvent while InteractiveChat hooks legacy AsyncPlayerChatEvent — parallel pipelines cause `[item]` placeholders to be stripped. Fix: set `Chat_Listener_Priority: LOWEST` (drop `-MODERN`) in settings.yml, then full server restart (this key cannot be `/chc reload`ed).
